unit.name: Single-stage and transformer detectors: YOLO and DETR
unit.description: Covers faster one-stage detection with YOLO and then shows how transformer encoder-decoder designs can be repurposed for detection with learned object queries.
unit.summary: To avoid the two-stage cost of R-CNN-style detectors, the lecture introduces single-stage detection and uses YOLO as the canonical example: the image is divided into an S x S grid, each cell predicts B bounding boxes plus objectness and class probabilities, and postprocessing uses thresholding and non-maximum suppression to keep the best detections [ts=2675s] [ts=2738s] [ts=2772s] [ts=2851s]. The segment then presents DETR as a transformer-based detector built from encoder tokens with positional encodings plus a decoder that receives learned object queries rather than proposal boxes [ts=2940s] [ts=2997s] [ts=3006s] [ts=3052s]. Each query outputs either a class and bounding box or a no-object prediction, supervision still uses class labels and box losses, and the instructor emphasizes that queries are trainable parameters rather than image patches; more queries can be used when more objects are expected [ts=3104s] [ts=3121s] [ts=3208s] [ts=3275s] [ts=3449s] [ts=3460s].

key_points.text: YOLO divides the image into an S x S grid for one-pass detection.
Each grid cell predicts B boxes together with objectness and class probabilities.
Thresholding and non-maximum suppression are used to keep the highest-confidence YOLO detections.
DETR reuses a transformer encoder-decoder architecture for object detection.
DETR uses learned object queries in the decoder to request up to N detected objects.
Each object query outputs either a class-plus-box prediction or a no-object label.
Object queries are learned parameters, not image patches or predefined boxes.

kp_catalog.name: Object detection as joint classification and localization
kp_catalog.description: Detection extends classification by predicting both an object category and bounding-box coordinates for each object.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because object detection as joint classification and localization formalizes how detection extends classification by predicting both an object category and bounding-box coordinates for each object, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: YOLO one-stage detection
kp_catalog.description: YOLO predicts boxes and classes for all grid cells in a single pass and then filters them with confidence-based postprocessing.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because yOLO one-stage detection clarifies yOLO predicts boxes and classes for all grid cells in a single pass and then filters them with confidence-based postprocessing, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: DETR object queries
kp_catalog.description: DETR uses learned decoder queries to emit a set of object predictions, each containing a class, a box, or a no-object output.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because dETR object queries clarifies dETR uses learned decoder queries to emit a set of object predictions, each containing a class, a box, or a no-object output, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
